summary: Russia launched a large-scale combined overnight attack on Ukraine with seven Iskander-M ballistic missiles and 189 strike drones, targeting industrial facilities in Kremenchuk, Odesa region, and Zaporizhzhia. A Russian MiG-31K Kinzhal carrier scrambled from Savastleyka airbase, triggering an air alert across the entire country. Ground operations continued near Krasny Lyman in DNR, where Russian forces are clearing terrain along the railway and advancing in the forest southwest of the city.
key_points: Overnight Russian attack launched 7 Iskander-M ballistic missiles and 189 strike drones; air defenses intercepted 3 missiles and 174 drones, per Air Force reports (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
MiG-31K Kinzhal carrier scrambled from Savastleyka airbase, prompting nationwide air alert due to the weapon's limited interceptability in most regions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68082).
Multiple UAV groups detected across northern, eastern, and southern axes: Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Odesa, Sumy, Poltava, and Chernihiv regions all under drone threat (Source: @kpszsu/66521).
Russian MoD claimed strike on an oil refinery in Kremenchuk, Poltava region; the city experienced a partial blackout following the attack (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/382569, @UkraineNow/82186).
Russian forces cleared terrain along the railway throughout Krasny Lyman in DNR and advanced in the forest belt southwest of the city (Source: @tass_agency/382603).
Ukraine conducted a prisoner exchange, releasing 160 military personnel — all captured in 2022 — from multiple front sectors including Mariupol and Azovstal (Source: @UkraineNow/82195).
Ukrainian Commander-in-Chief Syrsky stated Russia plans to increase FPV drone usage from 7,000 to 33,000 per day by year's end (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31193).
Ukrainian Foreign Minister Sybiga stated Belarus is not expected to enter the war, saying Ukraine would mirror any such move (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31191).

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a large-scale combined overnight attack on Ukraine with seven Iskander-M ballistic missiles and 189 strike drones, targeting industrial facilities in Kremenchuk, Odesa region, and Zaporizhzhia. A Russian MiG-31K Kinzhal carrier scrambled from Savastleyka airbase, triggering an air alert across the entire country. Ground operations continued near Krasny Lyman in DNR, where Russian forces are clearing terrain along the railway and advancing in the forest southwest of the city.

points: Ukrainian Air Force reported that air defenses intercepted 3 of 7 Iskander-M ballistic missiles and 174 of 189 strike drones during the overnight attack on June 26, 2026 (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).

A massive fire broke out at a warehouse in Glukhov, Sumy region, following a strike; State Emergency Service reported difficulty extinguishing due to ongoing threat of further attacks (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31188).

47th OMBr Strike UAV unit destroyed a Russian BM-21 Grad MLRS using the Striks system near the DKU, with strikes hitting during the moment of the enemy's salvo; location identified as outskirts of Snagost (Source: @DeepStateUA/23598).

Ukraine released 160 military prisoners of war in an exchange; all had been in captivity since 2022, including defenders of Mariupol, Azovstal, and personnel from Donetsk, Luhansk, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Kyiv, Chernihiv, and Sumy sectors (Source: @UkraineNow/82195).

Ukrainian Foreign Minister Sybiga stated Belarus is not expected to enter the war, asserting Ukraine would mirror any such move by Russia to drag Belarus into the conflict (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31191).
Commander-in-Chief Syrsky stated Russia plans to increase FPV drone usage on front lines from 7,000 per day to 33,000 by the end of the year (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31193).

President Zelensky reported over 9,500 people returned to Ukraine since the start of full-scale war, with 1,596 repatriated this year alone, and indicated further exchange stages are expected (Source: @UkraineNow/82211).

Russian MoD claimed a strike on an oil refinery in Kremenchuk, Poltava region; the city experienced a partial blackout as a result of the attack on industrial facilities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31189, @UkraineNow/82186).

Russian forces cleared terrain along the railway throughout Krasny Lyman in DNR and advanced in the forest belt southwest of the city, according to military expert Andrey Marochko (Source: @tass_agency/382603).

Russian forces eliminated a Ukrainian border detachment commander via a strike on a command post in Bely Kolodez, Kharkov region, according to Russian security sources cited by TASS (Source: @tass_agency/382572).
A MiG-31K fighter — carrier of the Kh-47M2 Kinzhal aeroballistic missile — scrambled from Savastleyka airbase, triggering a nationwide air alert in Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68082).
Russian Ambassador to the UN Trofimov stated the West does not intend to abandon sanctions against Russia in either the medium or long term (Source: @tass_agency/382599).
Putin and Lukashenko met at the Russian president's Valdai residence to discuss Union State agenda, trade-economic cooperation, and regional security, per Peskov (Source: @tass_agency/382573).

Multiple UAV groups targeted Kharkiv city and Kharkiv Oblast from the north and east, with specific sightings near Vilshany, Pisochyn, Solonitsivka, and BNR (Belgorod Oblast) heading toward Bogodukhiv (Source: @eRadarrua/98326, @kpszsu/66521, @eRadarrua/98322).
Russian forces struck Zaporizhzhia with ballistic missiles, injuring at least six people; a fire broke out at the strike site (Source: @UkraineNow/82190, @UkraineNow/82213).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on the city of Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/66527).
Eight groups of UAVs were detected heading toward Zaporizhzhia, with one group over the city near Khortytsia and Pivkolo (Source: @eRadarrua/98295).
Russian forces cleared terrain along the railway throughout Krasny Lyman in DNR and advanced in the forest belt southwest of the city (Source: @tass_agency/382603).
A KAB (corrected aerial bomb) was reported near Riptsi heading toward Zaporizhzhia Oblast, specifically Maliokaterynivka (Source: @eRadarrua/98319).
UAV activity reported on the border of Kyiv and Zhytomyr regions near Krasyatychy, indicating drone threats extending to Ukraine's central-northern areas (Source: @eRadarrua/98276).

Strike UAVs were detected on the border of Chernihiv and Sumy regions heading toward Poltava Oblast, with a reactive UAV from Sumy region heading directly toward Poltava city (Source: @kpszsu/66525, @kpszsu/66539).
UAVs were detected in eastern Sumy Oblast heading toward Buryn and Konotop, with specific sightings near Okhtyrka and Mykolaivka (Source: @kpszsu/66507, @eRadarrua/98330).

A massive fire broke out at a warehouse in Glukhov, Sumy region, following a strike; State Emergency Service reported difficulty extinguishing due to ongoing threat of further attacks (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31188).

UAVs launched from the Black Sea targeted southern Odesa Oblast, heading toward Tatarbunary, Vylkove, and Zatoka in multiple waves (Source: @kpszsu/66503, @kpszsu/66500, @kpszsu/66504, @kpszsu/66508).
Strike UAVs approached Zaporizhzhia from the south, with groups of attack UAVs from the southwestern direction heading toward the city (Source: @kpszsu/66503, @kpszsu/66524).
Odesa region was attacked, with one person injured; energy and civilian infrastructure was hit, leaving Vylkove and part of the community without power (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
Three people were injured in Zaporizhzhia as a result of a nighttime drone attack, with a fire breaking out at the strike location (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
Russia launched 7 Iskander-M ballistic missiles and 189 strike drones overnight; Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 3 missiles and 174 drones (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).

A MiG-31K — carrier of the Kh-47M2 Kinzhal aeroballistic missile — scrambled from Savastleyka airbase, triggering a nationwide air alert; the Kinzhal is noted as not interceptable in most regions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68082).
An air alert was declared across all of Ukraine at 19:09 Kyiv time on June 26, 2026, due to the MiG-31K scramble (Source: @eRadarrua/98282, @eRadarrua/98283).
Threat of strike UAVs was declared for Chernihiv, Sumy, Kharkiv, and Zaporizhzhia regions (Source: @kpszsu/66530).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on the city of Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/66527).

key_developments: Russia launched 7 Iskander-M ballistic missiles and 189 strike drones overnight; Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 3 missiles and 174 drones, with industrial facilities in Kremenchuk and Odesa region struck (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
MiG-31K Kinzhal carrier scrambled from Savastleyka airbase, triggering nationwide air alert; the Kh-47M2 missile is noted as not interceptable in most Ukrainian regions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68082).
Russian forces cleared terrain along the railway throughout Krasny Lyman in DNR and advanced in the forest belt southwest of the city (Source: @tass_agency/382603).
Ukraine released 160 military POWs, all captured in 2022, including defenders of Mariupol and Azovstal, from multiple front sectors (Source: @UkraineNow/82195).
47th OMBr Strike UAV unit destroyed a Russian BM-21 Grad MLRS using the Striks system near Snagost during the enemy's salvo (Source: @DeepStateUA/23598).
Commander-in-Chief Syrsky stated Russia plans to increase FPV drone usage from 7,000 to 33,000 per day by year's end (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31193).
Ukrainian Foreign Minister Sybiga stated Belarus is not expected to enter the war, asserting Ukraine would mirror any such move (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31191).
sections.threat_assessment: The combined ballistic missile and drone barrage on June 26 demonstrates Russia's continued capacity for large-scale strategic strikes across multiple Ukrainian regions simultaneously. The MiG-31K scramble with Kinzhal missiles represents an elevated air threat due to the weapon's reported limited interceptability. Russian ground forces maintain offensive pressure near Krasny Lyman in DNR while conducting infrastructure strikes on energy facilities in Odesa and Poltava regions. The planned Russian escalation of FPV drone usage to 33,000 per day signals a significant increase in attritional drone warfare that will challenge Ukrainian air defense capacity.
sections.regional_response: The European Commission proposed extending temporary protection for Ukrainian refugees through March 2028, though this will not apply to military-age men (23-60) or those already granted protection. Denmark announced it will stop granting asylum to Ukrainian men aged 23-60 subject to conscription, citing the need not to create legal loopholes weakening its own defense. Poland's relations with Ukraine show strain, with three additional Polish politicians declining Ukrainian awards in solidarity with Volyn massacre victims' families, following Law and Justice leader Kaczyński's earlier surrender of his order. Hungarian Prime Minister Péter Magyar refused to sign an EU request to open negotiations with Ukraine on other issues.
sections.intelligence_notes: The overnight attack pattern — combining ballistic missiles with mass drone salvos across northern, eastern, and southern axes — reflects a coordinated Russian strategy to overwhelm Ukrainian air defenses through simultaneous multi-vector threats. The use of the MiG-31K as a psychological and strategic escalation trigger, given its nationwide alert effect, appears designed to compound the operational impact of the broader strike campaign. The 47th OMBr's successful Striks strike on a Grad system near Snagost demonstrates continued Ukrainian counter-battery UAV capability. The prisoner exchange of 160 personnel, all from 2022, suggests ongoing diplomatic channels despite intensified combat operations.
